1: Metal plates underneath its fur protect it from harm. It replenishes its energy by basking in the sun's rays.
2: METALYNX stalk their prey in packs. Their brutally sharp tail blades are their main weapon.
3: RAPTORCH are energetic Pokemon that require constant attention, or they will start setting their surroundings on fire.
4: With a specialized saddle and reins, ARCHILLES can be ridden. Make sure you have earned its trust first.
5: ELETUX is capable of generating strong magnetic fields around its feet and tail that allow it to walk on water.
6: Electruxo can use the electromagnetic waves they generate as radar. This allows them to see even in pitch darkness.
7: The length of a Chyinmunk's stripes determines its authority in a group. If it spots a predator, it shrieks loudly to warn the others.
8: Kinetmunk digs intricate tunnels that can stretch for miles underground. They use small electrical pulses to relax their muscles, allowing them to run faster.
9: It defends itself by dazzling opponents by flashing its brightly colored plumage. They are known for their elaborate mating dances.
10: The blue feathers on its chest reflect light. They use flashes to communicate with one another in the forest canopy.
11: Its spectacular plumage makes it a frequent target of poachers. However, due to the heavy feathers, it's not a great flyer.
12: It shares characteristics with Bug and Mammal PokÃ©mon. Being in its presence has a calming effect.
13: It scatters pollen and seeds in order to help flowers grow. It always smells like flower petals.
14: Its presence causes plants to flourish. You can tell where it makes its nest because it is always surrounded by flowers.
15: Incredibly dense metals make up Barewl's body. They are much heavier than they look. They live in dark caves and feed on rocks and metal ore.
16: It is a stubborn PokÃ©mon that will always stand its ground, even against a much larger opponent. It uses its horn to dig for metal ore.
17: A gentle giant, Gararewl are peaceful herbivores but won't hesitate to defend themselves if attacked. They whip foes using the razor wire on their head.
18: Its snakelike body is almost always partially underground. The tunnels they dig can serve as pitfalls for the unwary traveler.
19: Terlard's two heads have completely independent brains and are prone to argument. That they ever manage to coordinate at all is a miracle.
20: Newbie trainers traveling through caves are advised to bring along antidotes against a TONEMY's poison, which causes great pain if it isn't treated immediately.
21: Unable to fly or move swiftly like TONEMY, TOFURANG instead catch their prey by exhaling a poison gas that makes Pokemon sluggish.
22: Its drill-tipped tail is used to burrow into the ground backwards. This PokÃ©mon is known to make its nest in complex shapes deep under the ground.
23: Said to be descended from a Dunsparce that climbed Sky Pillar. It is a powerful flyer and can alter the air pressure at will.
24: This PokÃ©mon is rarely seen walking because of its weight. Rather, it rolls to get anywhere. They are often found at the bottoms of ponds.
25: FOLEROG can't swim but they need to remain damp to survive, so they are found in grasslands next to rivers and lakes.
26: BLUBELROG are the masters of the calm water. They are very social and form communities in and around freshwater lakes.
27: Its swimming muscles are weak, so it is easily washed away by currents. In places where water pools, you can see Magikarp deposited there by the flow.
28: It is an extremely vicious and violent PokÃ©mon. When humans begin to fight, it will appear and burn everything to the ground with intensely hot flames.
29: Although Feleng is small and weak, it can manipulate other PokÃ©mon just by giving them a pleading look and meowing cutely.
30: Felunge are expert hunters, able to see in very low light and move without a sound. Many people keep them to deter pests such as CHYINMUNK.
31: Many trainers are disappointed when Felunge evolves because it gets fat and lazy. It can crush foes under its enormous body mass.
32: When it starts shaking and its nasal breathing turns rough, it's a sure sign of anger. However, since this happens instantly, there is no time to flee.
33: When it becomes furious, its blood circulation becomes more robust, and its muscles are made stronger. But it also becomes much less intelligent.
34: A natural leader of other PokÃ©mon, Empirilla have learned to control their rage and use it to increase their power in battle.
35: Owten are mainly seen at night, when they are looking for berries and other individuals of its species. They are known for their lovely singing voice.
36: Eshouten's incredible eyesight allows them to see clearly up to three miles away. If you see red circles in a tree at night, it is light reflected from their eyes.
37: This PokÃ©mon lives in ponds with clean water. It is known to ferry small PokÃ©mon across ponds by carrying them on the broad leaf on its head.
38: In the evening, it takes great delight in popping out of rivers and startling people. It feeds on aquatic moss that grows on rocks in the riverbed.
39: When it hears festive music, all the cells in its body become stimulated, and it begins moving in rhythm. It does not quail even when it faces a tough opponent.
40: If a SMORE approaches your picnic, it's not trying to steal your food, it just wants attention. Still, some people are afraid of them.
41: Its belly is filled with super hot lava that it sprays at its opponent through the nozzle on its abdomen. Its aim is precise.
42: This PokÃ©mon is known for its intelligence. It can't swim fast, but it hides among coral reefs, using its psychic powers to detect others.
43: Called "The sage of the sea", Brainoar is said to posess all the wisdom of the ocean. It is calm and dislikes fighting.
44: An EKANS curls itself up in a spiral while it rests. This position allows it to quickly respond to an enemy from any direction with a threat from its head.
45: This PokÃ©mon has a terrifically strong constricting power. It can even flatten steel oil drums. Once it wraps its body around its foe, escaping is impossible.
46: Tancoon are mischievous, and are known to steal food from small PokÃ©mon or journeying trainers.
47: In the wild Tanscure live in packs. They cooperate with their packmates to hunt for food and care for Tancoon.
48: SPONEE are covered by a bizarre spongelike material. When dropped, they bounce.
49: It fills its sponge-like skin with water and carries it into its hive. It can release all the stored water at once.
50: Pahar are intelligent and are commonly kept as pets. They can even be taught to talk, although not to the degree of CHATOT.
51: Palij's curiously-shaped feathers allow it to create friction in the air as it flaps its wings. Sparks trail off of its feathers when it flies.
52: Every year, massive flocks of Pahar and Palij migrate from Tandor to the Sevii Islands. A Pajay leads each one.
53: The spines on its back stand rigid when charged with electricity. It is popular with children and young trainers.
54: It can retract its legs into its body and appear to be an ordinary rock. Stories say they arrived on an asteroid.
55: It levitates in the air with powerful psychic energy. The crystals on its body flash many colors when it meets another of its kind.
56: Astronite can exist anywhere, even in the vacuum of space. They are said to roam across the universe, orbiting stars and colonizing asteroid fields.
57: Its fluffy wool rubs together and builds a static charge. The more energy is charged, the more brightly the lightbulb at the tip of its tail glows.
58: Its fleece quality changes to generate strong static electricity with a small amount of wool. The bare, slick parts of its hide are shielded against electricity.
59: It gives off so much light that it can be seen even from space. People in the old days used its light to send signals back and forth with others far away.
60: Its wool is prized as a fiber for extra-rugged clothing. However, it is much harder to raise than its docile cousin MAREEP.
61: Now that it is able to stand on two legs, it can use more kicking and punching attacks. It loves to fight dirty and use underhanded tactics.
62: A master at fighting dirty, Baariette like to ensure that their foes can never oppose them again.
63: Related to SMORE and SPONEE, TRICWE is very hyperactive and loves nothing better than sneaking up behind trainers and scaring them.
64: Harylect gather near sources of electricity and therefore are drawn to cities and power plants. They can plug their antennae into a wall socket to recharge.
65: At this stage of development, Costraw's telekinetic powers are weak. It instead latches onto its foes and sucks out their life force.
66: Trawpint uses its telekinesis to move objects behind its foe, preventing them from escaping. It latches on with its sucker and drains life energy.
67: Although small, it is very brave. It won't hesitate to protect weaker Pokemon in danger. They can often be heard howling at the moon.
68: It rises with the moon to do battle with its sworn nemesis, Vilucard. It has a strong sense of justice and will protect those who cannot defend themselves.
69: Called the "Tiny Master of Evil", it tries to cause discord but often ends up messing up and fleeing in embarrassment.
70: It constantly plots dastardly schemes to spread chaos and fear. However, its plans are always foiled by Herolune.
71: It has a flotation sac that is like an inflatable collar. It floats on water with its head out.
72: It floats using its well-developed flotation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.
73: Modrille hate being confined within any space and will always drill their way out of it. Trainers are advised to keep spare PokÃ© balls on hand.
74: Often used as beasts of burden, a Drilgann can tunnel many miles in a single day without getting tired. Its claws can be spun separately to drill with precision.
75: It glides without making a single sound. It grasps the face of its foe using its hind and large front claws, then stabs with its poison barb.
76: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!
77: It digs branching holes in caves using its sharp claws in search of food--raw gems. A Sableye lurks in darkness and is seen only rarely.
78: When COCARAN retracts its eyes and legs, it is almost indistinguishable from a coconut. Their shells are very buoyant, so they float in water.
79: Unlike their preevolutions, CARARALM cannot swim, so they stay on land. The height of their palm tree indicates how close they are to evolving.
80: Cocancer bury themselves in the sand and are indistinguishable from ordinary palm trees. When prey walks by, they leap out and crush it with massive claws.
81: They prefer unpolluted southern seas. Their coral branches lose their color and deteriorate in dirty water.
82: Their bodies are a host to a wide variety of other species. In return for a home, they give CORSOREEF the ability to inflict status effects on its foes.
83: Tubjaw are able to unhinge their jaws in order to swallow prey larger than their head. Their teeth are sharper than razors.
84: A fierce, deep-sea hunter, this PokÃ©mon is particularly vengeful towards humankind and will tear up fishing nets.
85: It dissolves minerals in sand and uses them to form a hard carapace on its back. It lives in coastal regions.
86: The colorful patterns on its shell makes them desirable to collectors. Their cast-off shells are often used in jewelry.
87: Its shell has hardened into a nearly unbreakable fortress. It shoots water from the turrets on its back.
88: NUPIN is the largest single-celled organism on the planet. It obtains energy from photosynthesis.
89: The energy that GELLIN generates from photosynthesis is converted into electromagnetic pulses. It uses these as a means of propulsion.
90: They go wherever the wind takes them. On rainy days, their bodies are heavier, so they take shelter beneath big trees.
91: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.
92: A Misdreavus frightens people with a creepy, sobbing cry. It apparently uses its red spheres to absorb the fear of foes as its nutrition.
93: Its cries sound like incantations. Those hearing it are tormented by headaches and hallucinations.
94: The appendage at the end of its tail is its primary form of attack, and it is able to deftly manipulate objects with it as if it were a hand.
95: A PokÃ©mon highly sensitive to the changes in seasons, Glaslug come and go with the warm undersea currents.
96: GLAVINUG are rare but not hard to trace, due to the ice crystals they leave behind when they swim.
97: A peculiar life-form that hovers above the ground with a mysterious force. Rumors say they came from outer space.
98: Some people suspect that S51-A, Beeheyem, and Clefable are conspiring to invade the planet. No one is safe.
99: It is highly sensitive to sound and can perfectly replicate any sound it hears, even human speech. It loves to hear fast, rhythmic music.
100: It uses massive sonic booms to attack from a distance. An encounter with one will leave your ears ringing for days.
101: The size of the flame on Flager's tail indicates if it is close to evolving. Some Flager will intentionally eat flammable items to make it bigger.
102: Inflagetah's are some of the fastest known PokÃ©mon on earth, able to outrun even a Rapidash. They can reach speeds up to 200 MPH.
103: A Pokemon said to be the product of genetic testing. The white spot on its back is its weak point.
104: Normally passive unless provoked, CHIMACONDA inject their prey with a paralytic venom and then wrap it in their tails, squeezing the life out.
105: It hides its true face behind a wooden mask. They will often convene in order to dance elaborate dances.
106: It produces deep bellows and thumps from within its mask that can be heard for miles. It can summon all the Tikiki in an area and together they make music by drumming on their masks.
107: They inhabit mucky bogs and swamps. Although they are somewhat slow and weak in battle, they can extend their jaws rapidly to deliver a powerful blow.
108: A swift and fierce hunter. They can vibrate their wings at supersonic speeds in flight. They use their harpoon-shaped tails to skewer prey.
109: Chicoatl are shy, but can be affectionate. They are said to be related to both dragon and bird Pokemon.
110: Quetzoral often wrap themselves around long branches of trees and fall asleep. They dine on nectar from flowers.
111: Early natives of Baykal worshipped Coatlith as gods. They are fiercely devoted to protecting nature, and will attack anyone who defiles it.
112: A Mechanical Dragon PokÃ©mon that is often used to help construction workers. Its jaws can crumple steel with ease.
113: A Pokemon that roamed the tundra thousands of years ago. They used their small tusks to dig up berries and roots under the ice.
114: It charges forward with the unstoppable force of a glacier, and its punch can shatter solid rock. They were hunted to extinction by early humans.
115: A very lazy Pokemon that lived thousands of years ago. They would climb to the top of trees and wait to be struck by lightning.
116: An ancient Pokemon known for its lightning-fast strikes. It used its incredibly sharp claws to shave the bark off of trees.
117: They migrate all over the ocean, mainly at the poles but occasionally moving to warmer waters. What lies beneath the surface is a mystery.
118: A favored PokÃ©mon of Ninja clans. They propel themselves backwards through the water to attack with their sharp tails.
119: They are the bane of many fishermen as their bladed tails often cut fishing lines. They can change direction rapidly in the water.
120: It often wields Frynai or Saidine as weapons. The sword on its head is made from steel folded 10,000 times.
121: They gather together on rocky shoals and sing together as a harmony. Their human-like voices can be misleading because they sound like a child.
122: Their song is hypnotic, and all who hear it are immobilized. Legends tell of shipwrecks that were caused when the sailors were captivated by their song.
123: In the past, rather than using perfume, royal ladies carried a Spritzee that would waft a fragrance they liked.
124: Its scent is so overpowering that, unless a Trainer happens to really enjoy the smell, he or she will have a hard time walking alongside it.
125: It has the ability to both cause and cure disease. Using its claws, it injects pathogens into its foes.
126: It is related to BUNEARY, but is much more crafty. It conceals itself using trickery, so many people don't believe it exists.
127: It combines illusion and trickery with physical strength. Because it can only be seen by drunk people, it was considered an imaginary PokÃ©mon.
128: This Pokemon has the ability to create an identical copy of itself or its opponent. These copies only last a few minutes before fading away.
129: An Eevee has an unstable genetic makeup that suddenly mutates due to its environment. Radiation from various stones causes this PokÃ©mon to evolve.
130: Vaporeon underwent a spontaneous mutation and grew fins and gills that allow them to live underwater. They have the ability to freely control water.
131: Its cells generate weak power that is amplified by its fur's static electricity to drop thunderbolts. The bristling fur is made of electrically charged needles.
132: Flareon's fluffy fur releases heat into the air so that its body does not get excessively hot. Its body temperature can rise to a maximum of 1,650 degrees F.
133: An Espeon is extremely loyal to any trainer it considers to be worthy. It is said to have developed precognitive powers to protect its trainer from harm.
134: Umbreon evolved from exposure to the moon's energy. It lurks in darkness and waits for its foes to move. The rings on its body glow when it leaps to attack.
135: Just like a plant, it uses photosynthesis. As a result, it is always enveloped in clear air.
136: As a protective technique, it can completely freeze its fur to make its hairs stand like needles.
137: It sends a soothing aura from its ribbonlike feelers to calm fights.
138: One of the only non-feral Nuclear Pokemon. It's theorized that its bond with its trainer when it was an Eevee allows it to stay in control.
139: They use the paint-like substance on their tails to mark their territory. They are considered pests as they will paint over brand new buildings.
140: They spray paint to create signs that only Raffiti can understand. Their knowledge of color theory has led some to consider them artists on par with humans.
141: They spend most of their time standing so still they are often mistaken for statues. They perch on the rooftops of Venisi, guarding it from outside attackers.
142: It is capable of disguising itself as anything it likes. Although it appears to be a bird PokÃ©mon, this is just an illusion. No one has seen its true form.
143: A sinister and vengeful PokÃ©mon, it puts its foes into an eternal sleep and steals their souls to add to its tail.
144: Said to be the ghost of a Pokemon that died before its time. It guides lost souls into the afterlife using the light of its small star.
145: It comes out at night to suck the blood from sleeping prey. Its needles contain a numbing toxin that can incapacitate an enemy in minutes.
146: It does not understand the meaning of "mercy" and will ruthlessly attack with deadly toxins. It can shoot poisonous spines from its back.
147: The spirit of a PokÃ©mon that died inside its PokÃ© Ball. It has forgotten its original appearance so it takes the form of a Substitute decoy.
148: A malicious and dangerous PokÃ©mon, it seeks revenge against the trainer who abandoned it. Its shackles were reinforced in order to keep its violence in check.
149: It constantly wraps itself in its long tail to protect it from the cold. Despite this, it always seems to shiver as if it is freezing.
150: Its two tails are coated with a waxy material that allows it to glide over snow and ice. They are often seen hurtling down mountain at high speeds.
151: Its eyes are covered by a shield that protects it from blowing snow and ice. The one that can perform the wildest flips and tricks is deemed the leader.
152: The older they get, the sharper the ice spikes on their back become. Stepping on one buried under the snow is an unpleasant experience.
153: It encases itself in a hard shell of unmelting ice and hangs upside-down from the ceilings of Lanthanite Cave, awaiting evolution.
154: They leave a shimmering trail of ice crystals behind them as they fly. They are prized in many regions for their rarity and beauty.
155: This Pokemon's shaggy coat keeps it warm in freezing weather. They are sought after by hunters for their silky fur.
156: A gentle guardian of the mountains, this Pokemon will often escort lost travelers and Pokemon to safety by giving them a ride on its back.
157: It is able to fire a barrage of superheated blasts from its cannon. Despite its small size, it is a very capable predator.
158: Called "The Ranger of the Sea". Its reaction times are nigh-instantaneous and its aim with its pistol is excellent due to its highly advanced eyesight.
159: It lives within igneous crevasses at the bottom of the sea and absorbs geothermal energy. When prey comes close, it spits out scalding hot water.
160: A favorite PokÃ©mon of pirates. The bandanna it wears indicates which ship's crew it belongs to. It craves treasure and will hoard any gold and jewels it finds.
161: It requires seemingly no sustenance such as food or air. Although it can't swim, it will walk across the bottom of the sea until it makes it to shore.
162: Said to be the spirit of a mad pirate captain reincarnated as a PokÃ©mon. Its spectral flames are icy cold to the touch.
163: It flaps its wings to generate electricity, which it stores in its tail.  It channels the stored energy into its legs to run faster.
164: Its hide is studded with pieces of solid gold. Because of how valuable this Pokemon is on the black market, its population dwindled until it became extinct in the wild.
165: Its enormously heavy arms are made of solid gold. This PokÃ©mon has developed extraordinarily strong muscles to hold them up.
166: Its body exudes a sticky slime that protects its soft flesh.  It traps prey in the slime and delivers a lightning shock through its whiskers.
167: The slime covering its body is toxic and corrosive. Between that and its electric bite, it has no known predators. They proliferate in contaminated areas.
168: A species that spontaneously appeared in irradiated zones. It is thought to have hatched from a corrupted Egg.
169: A bizarre life form which thrives in irradiated fields of nuclear waste. It is a parasitic creature that latches on to any prey and suck out its life force.
170: It is known to spit radioactive acid capable of melting through steel. It is a ruthless predator that will attack any living creature, human or PokÃ©mon.
171: They appear in the aftermath of nuclear fallout. They feed on radiation and can clean an irradiated area over time.
172: Its simple body structure allows it to survive intense levels of radiation. They multiply rapidly and will eat anything in sight.
173: Its small stature, gentle demeanor and delicate features make it beloved by young children. A popular line of toys is based on it.
174: The hair in its mane is made of gossamer-thin threads and is impossibly soft. It will only allow those it trusts to touch it.
175: Its powers are strongest at night when the moon is not visible. Only a veteran Trainer can tame one well enough to ride it.
176: Simply being around a Luxi is said to increase one's fortune. Ceramic statues of them are good luck charms.
177: It polishes its jade tail until it glimmers with lustrous sheen. A clipping of this PokÃ©mon's hair is said to bring good luck for 30 days.
178: A PokÃ©mon so rare and gentle, simply seeing one is said to bless someone with good fortune for a whole year.
179: A mysterious life-form that emerged one day out of a computer. When it unites with its twin, its powers multiply in strength.
180: A highly intelligent Pokemon capable of traveling through cyberspace. Found in pairs alongside its twin, Praseopunk.
181: These Pokemon live in packs in mountainous regions. They will often headbutt each other to improve their strength.
182: It uses its large, flexible tails to balance itself while it deals a series of rapid kicks and punches to its opponent.
183: A mighty Pokemon that is spoken of in legends. It causes earthquakes by pounding the ground with its massive tails.
184: They gather together during thunderstorms. Their chirping sounds like static electricity.
185: It perches on rocky outcroppings and looks at the sky for many hours. They can foretell changes in the weather.
186: A fearsome Pokemon told about in legends. It has the power to summon thunderstorms at will.
187: A PokÃ©mon that is said to come from a separate plane of existence. It is very curious about the world around it and often gets into mischief as a result.
188: It acts like a soldier, obeying orders without hesitation. It seems to have no moral compass and will do whatever its master commands.
189: Its kind came out of a fiery chasm which contained an otherworldly portal. A furnance hotter than the sun burns within its chest. If it burns you, the pain will never fade.
190: The queen of all bugs in Tandor. She lurks deep within the Anthell, sleeping for a year in between broods.
191: Garlikid believes itself to be a savior of all Pokemonkind. It will pick fights with anyone it considers villainous.
192: Called the "Boiling Wrath". Heat from its scales causes the water around it to boil. It attacks ships, melting the hull upon contact.
193: Called the "Unstoppable Spear". The ice horn on its head can easily penetrate solid steel. It battles BAITATAO and KRAKANAO for dominance over the sea.
194: Called the "Inescapable Maelstrom". It causes massive whirlpools that drag everything - ships, PokÃ©mon and people alike - into its bottomless maw.
195: Legends say that it helped form part of the Tandor region. Along with its brothers Actan and Heatran, it placed metallic ores in the planet's crust.
196: One of the Legendary Pokemon that formed the Tandor Region. It taught humans about the use of radioactive metals.
197: An artificial PokÃ©mon created to be a source of unlimited energy. The more radioactive fuel it consumes, the greater its power becomes. At max potential, it can split atoms.
198: W H I T E L I G H T S H I N E S
199: B L A C K S H A D E P R O T E C T S
200: A mysterious Pokemon that appears to be a cross between PLUSLE and ARTICUNO. In legends, it visited artists and gave them ideas for their next masterpiece.
201: Deep in the night, your shadow cast by a streetlight may suddenly overtake you. It is actually a Gengar running past you, pretending to be your shadow.
